sanskrit:
युञ्जन्नेवं सदात्मानं योगी विगतकल्मषः।
सुखेन ब्रह्मसंस्पर्शमत्यन्तं सुखमश्नुते॥
transliteration:
yuïjann evaà sadätmänaà yogé vigata-kalmañaù |
sukhena brahma-saàsparçam atyantaà sukham açnute ||
literal_meaning: Ever-uniting thus the self, the yogī—freed from blemish—easily tastes the endless bliss of contact with Brahman.
contextual_meaning: When attention is stabilized in the silent core of awareness, psychological ‘noise’ (guilt, rumination, craving) dissolves; what remains is an effortless, non-addictive joy that feels like coming home to one’s deepest identity.
psychological_state: Post-integrative clarity: the nervous system has shifted from threat-scanning to self-referential rest, producing a non-stimulant, non-conditional happiness.
trigger_scenario: After months of daily mindfulness practice, a recovering addict notices that the urge to self-soothe with substances simply does not arise; the ‘gap’ between urge and action has become a field of quiet contentment.
anchor_text: Rest in quiet awareness; let inner noise fade and feel the natural joy of being.
